message:
test(fcp-core): pin CapabilityGrant serde shape (flywheel_connectors-g5oxv)

Bead asks for ConnectorEntitlement serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip. fcp-core
has no type literally named ConnectorEntitlement. The closest
"entitlement" analogue is CapabilityGrant (capability.rs:1465) — a
single capability grant within a token, the documented "entitlement"
unit:

  CapabilityGrant {
      capability: CapabilityId,
      operation: Option<OperationId>,  // skip_serializing_if = None
  }

Used in policy_golden_vectors.rs fixtures but NOT yet pinned for its
own serde shape.

Coverage:
- JSON shape with operation Some — 2 fields (capability + operation)
  as bare strings
- JSON shape omits operation when None via skip_serializing_if —
  exactly 1 field present
- operation defaults to None when missing from wire form (the
  symmetric counterpart of skip_serializing_if for round-trip)
- JSON round-trip preserves both shapes
- CBOR round-trip preserves both shapes
- Equality semantics: structurally identical grants equal; distinct
  capability or operation produces unequal grants; Some(operation)
  differs from None
- Vec<CapabilityGrant> preserves insertion order through both JSON
  and CBOR round-trip; reversed order produces distinct wire bytes
- Distinct capability and operation axes produce distinct JSON
  serializations
- Cross-format consistency: JSON and CBOR decode to same grant in
  both Some and None operation cases

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i5iv4","title":"[REVIEW-P2][HIGH] fcp-registry TUF/Sigstore gate still trusts caller-forgeable verification flags","description":"crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:304-314 makes SupplyChainEvidence a public struct with public tuf_verified/sigstore_verified booleans, crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:932-937 accepts Option<&SupplyChainEvidence> directly from the caller, and crates/fcp-registry/src/lib.rs:1724-1739 now treats those booleans as the entire oracle for require_tuf/require_sigstore. Bug scenario: any embedding code can instantiate SupplyChainEvidence { tuf_verified: true, sigstore_verified: true, ..Default::default() } without running a verifier adapter and still satisfy the supposedly real verification gate, so c046efde replaces a presence-only bypass with a caller-asserted-bool bypass. Suggested fix: make the verified fields private and construct SupplyChainEvidence only from verifier result types (or perform TUF/Sigstore verification inside RegistryVerifier instead of trusting caller-supplied booleans).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:15:19Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.079149Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:56.078898Z","close_reason":"Made SupplyChainEvidence::tuf_verified and sigstore_verified fields PRIVATE. Added constructor-only promotion paths: with_tuf_verification_result(&TufVerificationResult) and with_sigstore_verification_result(&SigstoreVerificationResult) that only flip the flag when result.verified == true. Added pub getters tuf_verified() / sigstore_verified(). Test-only escape hatch mark_tuf_verified_for_tests() / mark_sigstore_verified_for_tests() gated behind #[cfg(any(test, feature = 'test-mocks'))] so downstream release builds cannot reach them. Migrated all 2 production call sites (fwc/supply_chain_cmd.rs, fwc/main.rs) + 4 integration test sites (fcp-registry/tests/no_mock_integration.rs) + 2 internal pcmm8 tests to the new API. Added DenyReason::IpNotAllowed handling to fwc/net_cmd.rs rule_id_for + suggestion_for arms. 2 new regression tests supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_tuf_verification_result + supply_chain_evidence_refuses_failed_sigstore_verification_result confirm failed verification results do NOT promote the flag. Verified: 264 lib tests pass (was 262 + 2 new), 88 integration tests pass, 563 fcp-sandbox lib tests pass. Pre-existing base_manifest_toml fixture-drift failure (98 tests) is unrelated and reproduces on HEAD without these changes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","title":"TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests (FCP-XXXX Codes, Retry Semantics, AI Hints)","description":"# TEST: Error Taxonomy Unit Tests


## Goal
Validate the error taxonomy is stable, code-complete, and machine-actionable (retry semantics + hints) with golden vectors.

## Scope
Comprehensive tests for error code system and recovery hints.

## Test Categories

### Error Code Ranges
- [ ] FCP-1xxx (protocol errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-2xxx (auth/capability errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-3xxx (zone errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-4xxx (store/mesh errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-5xxx (connector errors) mapped correctly
- [ ] FCP-9xxx (internal errors) mapped correctly

### Error Structure
- [ ] All errors have stable `code` string
- [ ] All errors have human-readable `message`
- [ ] All errors have `retryable` flag
- [ ] Retryable errors have `retry_after_ms`
- [ ] Errors include `details` when applicable

### AI Recovery Hints
- [ ] Top 20 failure modes have ai_recovery_hint
- [ ] Hints are actionable and specific
- [ ] Hints don't leak secrets
- [ ] Hints are stable across versions

### Error Serialization
- [ ] Errors serialize to canonical CBOR
- [ ] Errors deserialize correctly
- [ ] Unknown error codes handled gracefully
- [ ] Forward compatibility for new codes

### Retry Semantics
- [ ] Retryable errors retry correctly
- [ ] Non-retryable errors fail immediately
- [ ] Retry budget is respected
- [ ] Exponential backoff applied

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/errors/`
- `error_protocol.cbor`
- `error_auth.cbor`
- `error_zone.cbor`
- `error_retryable.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test_name": "test_error_taxonomy",
  "phase": "execute",
  "correlation_id": "uuid",
  "error_code": "FCP-2101",
  "retryable": false,
  "ai_hint_present": true,
  "result": "pass"
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- All error codes are tested
- AI hints exist for common failures
- Error serialization is stable

Tests are inline in fcp-core/src/error.rs. All 204 tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["errors","fcp2","mvp","testing","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.29","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i7nk","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T18:31:41Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-i8175","title":"TEST-CONFORMANCE-13: capability token instance-binding promote semantics + replay defense","description":"CapabilityToken<UnboundVerified>::promote_with_instance has three documented behaviors that form the replay-defense contract: (1) match -> BoundVerified, (2) mismatch -> ZoneViolation, (3) NO instance_id claim -> promote unconditionally (legacy 'universally applicable' tokens). Cases (1) and (2) are covered by jkcka_gateway_connector_handoff in fcp-host/tests, but the instance-agnostic case is NOT tested anywhere even though it's a deliberate security tradeoff. Plus the consuming 'self' signature means a single UnboundVerified token can be promoted only once - a typestate-level replay defense worth pinning at runtime too. Add capability_promote_with_instance_conformance.rs covering all 3 documented behaviors plus the cross-path equivalence (verify_bound directly = verify_unbound + promote). cc_2 domain (fcp-conformance + fcp-core).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:19:03.808130Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T21:20:45.749502Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T21:20:45.748981Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["capability","conformance","tests"],"comments":[{"id":3034,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-i8175","author":"jemanuel","text":"4a93f74c adds capability_promote_with_instance_conformance.rs with 7 tests pinning the three documented promote_with_instance branches (match/mismatch/instance-agnostic) plus cross-path equivalence (verify_bound vs verify_unbound+promote), claim preservation, ZoneViolation variant mapping, and the previously-untested third branch where an instance-agnostic token promotes unconditionally and against multiple different instances independently. All 7 pass in <0.01s. Complements jkcka_gateway_connector_handoff (which covers match+mismatch only).","created_at":"2026-04-27T21:20:45Z"}]}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-ia4r8","title":"Pin fcp-core EnforcementOutcome roundtrip","description":"Add tests in crates/fcp-core/tests/ pinning EnforcementOutcome serde JSON+CBOR roundtrip.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"cod6","created_at":"2026-04-29T03:00:33.147046Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-29T03:14:49.910125Z","closed_at":"2026-04-29T03:14:49.909841Z","close_reason":"Pinned enforcement outcome JSON and CBOR roundtrips for CheckOutcome; verified with rch exec -- cargo test -p fcp-core","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ibntq","title":"[REVIEW-A9][MEDIUM] fcp-sandbox/wasi.rs:1261: missing writable preopens widen file grants into directory authority","description":"prepare_preopened_dir() rejects existing file preopens but auto-creates any missing writable path with create_dir_all(path) and mounts it as a directory. Repro: declare a missing writable path like /tmp/fcp-secret.json, call WasiRuntime::new(...).create_store(), and the guest receives writable directory authority under /tmp/fcp-secret.json/. Manifest sandbox path validation also allows ambiguous absolute file-like strings, so this bypasses the existing file-only guard. Fix: fail closed on missing writable preopens unless the schema distinguishes directories from file grants.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-26T23:26:04.064406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-26T23:30:02.686682Z","closed_at":"2026-04-26T23:30:02.686435Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of jgtlf","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","title":"[MOR/C2.5] Establish quarterly claims-vs-reality debiasing reports process","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:33:13.740427Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:43.660396Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:43.659627Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["debiasing","documentation","process"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1h6ah","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:32.602054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1oj85","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:30.298064Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":2638,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"WHAT: Establish a process for quarterly claims-vs-reality reports. Format: for each claim in prior report, record current status, evidence, and delta. Publish in docs/quarterly/. This normalizes incompleteness and prevents bi-annual shock when claims are found to be stale. WHY: L2 Debiasing recommended institutional debiasing practices. Multi-agent development amplifies completion bias (agents optimize for task completion metrics, not honest assessment). Without periodic reality checks, documentation drift compounds over time. WHERE: Create docs/quarterly/ directory. Create template. First report covers the MOR analysis findings reconciliation. ACCEPTANCE: (1) Template exists. (2) First quarterly report produced. (3) Process documented in AGENTS.md or CONTRIBUTING.md. ESTIMATED EFFORT: 1 day to set up template; 1 day per quarterly report. DEPENDS ON: C2.4 (claims audit) — first quarterly report is the claims audit output.","created_at":"2026-04-08T03:34:18Z"},{"id":2669,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-icndt","author":"jemanuel","text":"C2.5 complete. Created docs/quarterly/TEMPLATE.md, produced inaugural 2026-Q2 report, added quarterly debiasing process to AGENTS.md. All 16 features verified baseline. Commit 70978239.","created_at":"2026-04-11T00:17:36Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-id5bl","title":"TEST-FUZZ-X: ReplayWindow sliding-bitmap state-machine fuzz","description":"fcp_protocol::session::ReplayWindow (session.rs:720-797) is the per-session sliding-bitmap replay-protection for FCPS frame seq numbers. Distinct from HelloReplayWindow (p5ams) which is a (from,nonce) FIFO. ReplayWindow.check / check_and_update gate the per-frame MAC verification to prevent CPU-exhaustion attacks from replays. Properties: reject seq=0; accept first non-zero seq; reject immediate replay; reject in-window already-seen; reject out-of-window (too old); future seq updates highest_seq + shifts bitmap; check ⇔ check_and_update agreement on the same state. Once-gated anchors: window_size=128 boundary (seq=highest-127 in, seq=highest-128 out), bitmap-shift correctness on large jumps. Acceptance: fuzz/fuzz_targets/replay_window.rs with model-driven fuzz + 3 anchors. cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"test","assignee":"SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:34:03.164170Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-27T22:35:37.579481Z","closed_at":"2026-04-27T22:35:37.578960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":3101,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-id5bl","author":"jemanuel","text":"Shipped in commit 53900f5e. fuzz/fuzz_targets/replay_window.rs — 5 properties (seq=0 reject, check purity, check⇔check_and_update agreement, immediate-replay reject, highest_seq monotonicity) + 3 once-gated anchors (window_size=128 inclusive boundary at diff=127 vs reject at diff=128, large-jump bitmap clear, immediate replay). Distinct from p5ams HelloReplayWindow (handshake-layer FIFO). cargo +nightly check + clippy -D warnings clean.","created_at":"2026-04-27T22:35:37Z"}]}
